briefing.headline: Crank it — pre-spawn clear water calls for finesse cranking on key structure.
briefing.gameplan:
In pre-spawn with clear water and post frontal conditions, cranking presentations shine.

KVD: Fish the Red Eye Shad like a worm or jig, not just cast-and-reel. Rip it out of cover, let it flutter down — that swimming shimmy on the fall is the magic. The colder it is, the slower you fish. Downsize to 1/4oz on ultra-shallow flats (1-2ft). Upsize line to 17-20lb fluoro to slow the fall and

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: KVD: Fish the Red Eye Shad like a worm or jig, not just cast-and-reel. Rip it out of cover, let it flutter down — that swimming shimmy on the fall is the magic. The colder it is, the slower you fish.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: During spring the prime depth zone on most lakes is 6 feet or less; the Jabber Jaw Hybrid Squarebill dives 3-5 feet which is the right zone for most fisheries across the country.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: On flat fisheries bass stay in the shallow zone a long time before spawning, but on highland reservoirs they may stage in 6-10 feet before moving up; crankbait selection should match the lake type and depth zone.

KVD: 'I pick up my fishing pace considerably as the water hits around 52°F. I may run down a bank quickly throwing a lipless crankbait or spinnerbait, then pause to pitch a jig at submerged logs or brushpiles.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal pre-spawn fish pull back to 6-12ft staging areas. They won't commit shallow after the front. Target the first breakline off spawning flats and key on deeper structure near shallow spawning zones.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clarity decreases from wind or rain, upsize lures and switch to brighter colors
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
